mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound reduces the equity risk premium discount rate on a persistent basis, producing a structural tailwind for S&P 500 forward returns as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%. This is a continuous trend effect, not event-triggered.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.18 as of t-1mo, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns during near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not statistically higher than the full-sample average (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 9.0% and remains elevated, equity markets price in extended labor market slack and weak consumption capacity, generating negative forward S&P 500 returns until a confirmed improvement (three consecutive monthly declines) reverses the signal.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE > 9.0% for at least 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: In periods where UNRATE > 9.0% for 2+ months, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or rank IC < 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in HOUST and stagnation in RSAFS signals a synchronized weakening of household demand across both durable (housing) and non-durable (retail) channels, which forward S&P 500 returns fail to price in over a quarterly horizon, producing negative subsequent returns. Neither series alone captures this; the joint deterioration is required.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines >5% over 3 months AND RSAFS month-over-month growth < 0.2%
failure_condition: Joint HOUST/RSAFS weakening triggers fail to precede negative 60d SPX returns in over 55% of historical instances, or the interaction effect's incremental rank IC over either single-series model is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock industrial recovery regime, sustained month-over-month gains in INDPRO signal re-accelerating manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which forward equity markets reward with positive returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings expectations for cyclicals improve.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=92.5354 as of t-2mo, rising from 2009 trough
failure_condition: In the post-2009-trough recovery regime, positive INDPRO momentum periods do not produce forward 60d SPX returns exceeding the full-sample mean, or rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows within this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A short-horizon positive surprise in PAYEMS combined with a simultaneous downtick in UNRATE produces an immediate risk-on repricing in equities over the following week, as both series jointly confirming labor market strength reduces recession-tail-risk pricing more than either signal alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month increase > 50,000 AND UNRATE decreases in the same month
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS/UNRATE improvement triggers are followed by positive 5d SPX returns in fewer than 55% of instances, or the joint-signal rank IC does not exceed either univariate signal's IC.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL month-over-month changes turn negative or near-zero for multiple consecutive months, deflation risk fears cause equity markets to price in weaker nominal earnings growth and policy uncertainty, generating negative forward S&P 500 returns until inflation readings stabilize above a positive threshold.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change < 0.1% for 2+ consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=217.597 as of t-1mo, low inflation environment
failure_condition: During low/negative CPI momentum periods, forward 20d SPX returns are not significantly more negative than baseline (p>0.05), or rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month growth in HOUST reflects improving household confidence and forward-looking demand for durable investment, which continuously predicts modestly positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week regardless of regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST trend and forward 5d SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive HOUST momentum periods do not show higher average forward returns than negative momentum periods (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-lower-bound monetary regime, rising INDPRO combined with FEDFUNDS remaining pinned near zero jointly signal that monetary accommodation is successfully translating into real output gains without triggering rate-hike fears, amplifying the positive equity response beyond what either series predicts alone over a quarterly horizon.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with industrial recovery
failure_condition: Joint FEDFUNDS/INDPRO trigger periods produce forward 60d SPX returns no better than the INDPRO-only signal's returns, or interaction-adjusted rank IC does not exceed 0.03 over rolling 2-year windows.
